published_prior_art.cite: BreunigJohannes2009LocalNPIR (https://arxiv.org/abs/0902.2103) and CaiLow2005AdaptationConfidenceIntervals (https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0503662) give the exact-solution/Gaussian convex-class linear-functional modulus machinery; ShenKallusMeunierZenatiGrettonBibaut2026NoSolutionNPIV (https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.24660) supplies the no-solution target but not this converse.

BreunigJohannes2009LocalNPIR (https://arxiv.org/abs/0902.2103) already treats source and representer ellipsoids for exact-solution NPIR linear functionals; the no-solution realized-vs-envelope distinction is not stated there.

BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2022StronglyIdentifiedWeaklyIdentified (https://arxiv.org/abs/2208.08291): root-n regularity is characterized by the dual/Riesz representer condition; in the singular basis this is sum_j psi_j^2/sigma_j^2 < infinity.

ChenReiss2011RateOptimalityIllPosedEconometrics (https://arxiv.org/abs/0709.2003) and HallHorowitz2006NonparametricMethodsIV (https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0603130) give mild/severe NPIV rate algebra, while BreunigJohannes2009LocalNPIR gives linear-functional ellipsoid rates for the exact-solution target.

BreunigJohannes2009LocalNPIR (https://arxiv.org/abs/0902.2103) and BreunigJohannes2011AdaptiveFunctionalsNPIR (https://arxiv.org/abs/1109.0961) are exactly the solvable-case benchmark being recovered.

BreunigJohannes2011AdaptiveFunctionalsNPIR (https://arxiv.org/abs/1109.0961), CaiLow2006AdaptiveLinearFunctionals (https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0602299), and BreunigChen2021AdaptiveQuadraticNPIV (https://arxiv.org/abs/2101.12282) give the ordered/Lepski adaptation templates and the same log-loss regimes for classical targets, not the no-solution projection functional.

novelty_flags.label: thm:rootn-frontier / comparator_promise_table
novelty_flags.one_line: The local-Riesz root-n iff is already the Bennett et al. dual-solution condition in singular coordinates, so it cannot be advertised as a strict tightening.

novelty_flags.label: related_work / BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2022StronglyIdentifiedWeaklyIdentified
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal says Bennett et al. only motivate the frontier, but their strongly-identified-functional condition is the same psi/sigma square-summability criterion after diagonalization.

soundness_flags.label: h^Pi / def:gaussian-nosolution-class
soundness_flags.one_line: A generic no-solution NPIV law need not have a least-squares minimizer; the core must include Shen et al.'s nonemptiness/domain condition or an equivalent residual decomposition before theta_psi is a law-defined functional.

soundness_flags.label: conj:adaptive-ordered-modulus
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed risk takes sup_t but compares it to bar_R_n(b^(t)) with t still free; it needs a per-t bound or a normalized sup_t risk ratio.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:gaussian-sequence / all rate statements
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The review accepts the singular-coordinate Gaussian experiment as an idealized reduction, not as a full i.i.d. NPIV minimax theorem.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: All modulus, envelope, profile-rate, and adaptive claims are conditional on the Gaussian singular-coordinate model.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oracle cutoff in thm:functional-modulus and prop:profile-rates
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The non-adaptive upper bounds use an oracle cutoff or an active frontier index; data-driven tuning is only conjectured in conj:adaptive-ordered-modulus.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Theorems 1-4 are oracle/minimax benchmark statements, not feasible adaptation claims.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: continuity and summability domains
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The fixed-functional and envelope claims are restricted to sum_j a_j^2 psi_j^2 < infinity and sum_j a_j^2 b_j^2 < infinity.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: No claim is accepted for discontinuous functionals or nonsummable source-representer products.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Shen et al.'s least-squares-solution nonemptiness condition explicitly, then prove a preliminary reduction from the no-solution residual decomposition to the stated Gaussian sequence coordinates.
Downgrade thm:rootn-frontier to a sanity corollary or reframe it as recovery of the Bennett et al. dual/Riesz condition rather than a new strict tightening.
Rewrite the adaptive conjecture as sup_t sup_P sup_psi E|error|/bar_R_{n,t}(b^(t)) <= C sqrt(log n), with separate clauses for no-loss severe and regular mildly ill-posed regimes.

To lift beyond field, replace the idealized sequence claim with either a full i.i.d. NPIV local-asymptotic reduction or a strict comparison against Shen et al.'s Tikhonov rate on explicit polynomial and exponential parameter regions.

recommended_next_step: Revise this angle: the no-solution modulus benchmark is plausibly field-level, but the target-domain condition and Bennett/root-n comparator claim must be repaired before Stage 0.
